title: `NOT field` incorrectly filters when `field` is an expression in a view over a PK field
description:
### Describe what's wrong
`NOT field` incorrectly prunes data when `field` is defined in a view as an expression that prunes data over a table's PK column. See below reproducer.
### Does it reproduce on the most recent release?
Yes
### How to reproduce
https://fiddle.clickhouse.com/3042ef38-7b7c-4883-955d-477ea1067bfc
```sql
CREATE TABLE t (val UInt8) ORDER BY (val);
CREATE VIEW v AS SELECT val, val = 0 AS is_zero FROM t;
-- When we apply a "NOT is_zero" filter on the view:
INSERT INTO t VALUES (0);  -- does not get pruned, but does not pass the "NOT is_zero" filter
INSERT INTO t VALUES (1);  -- gets pruned
INSERT INTO t VALUES (0), (2);  -- does not get pruned (as 0 exists in this part), and 2 passes the "NOT is_zero" filter: will return 2
-- See how the Condition is doubly negated "not((val not in [0, 0]))"
EXPLAIN indexes=1 SELECT * FROM v WHERE NOT is_zero;
/*
Expression ((Project names + (Projection + (WHERE + (Change column names to column identifiers + (Convert VIEW subquery result to VIEW table structure + (Materialize constants after VIEW subquery + (Project names + (Projection + (Change column names to column identifiers + (Project names + (Projection + Change column names to column identifiers))))))))))))
Filter (((WHERE + (Change column names to column identifiers + (Convert VIEW subquery result to VIEW table structure + (Materialize constants after VIEW subquery + (Project names + (Projection + (Change column names to column identifiers + (Project names + (Projection + Change column names to column identifiers))))))))))[split])
ReadFromMergeTree (default.t)
Indexes:
PrimaryKey
Keys:
val
Condition: not((val not in [0, 0]))
Parts: 2/3
Granules: 2/3
Search Algorithm: generic exclusion search
Ranges: 2
*/
-- Yields incorrect result (should be 2, 1)
SELECT val FROM v WHERE NOT is_zero;
/* 2 */
-- Inlining the view definition as a subquery yields the correct result
SELECT val FROM (SELECT val, val = 0 AS is_zero FROM t) WHERE NOT is_zero;
/* 2, 1 */
```
poc: CREATE TABLE t (val UInt8) ORDER BY (val);
CREATE VIEW v AS SELECT val, val = 0 AS is_zero FROM t;
key_bug_features: `NOT field` filters incorrectly with view expressions over PK
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE t2 (id UInt32, val Nullable(UInt8)) ENGINE = MergeTree() ORDER BY id;
CREATE VIEW v2 AS SELECT id, val, val IS NULL AS is_null FROM t2;
SELECT id FROM v2 WHERE NOT is_null;
